name: canonify-scenarios
description: How to write + run declarative Canonify scenarios (session.yaml) that prove an app slice end-to-end through the public Canon surface — live verification, not jsdom mocking. Read SKILL.md first, then deep-dive on structure, patterns, or running + debugging.

resources.title: Scenario structure
resources.description: The session.yaml document shape parsed by the harness SessionSchema (tests/scenarios/_harness/runner.ts): top-level name/principals/steps, the six step kinds (action / read / check / clock / orchestration / tick), the $ref grammar, and the expect matcher grammar (equals / matches / length / count / contains).

resources.title: Scenario patterns
resources.description: Worked, harness-valid examples: the CRM slice, an invoice FSM lifecycle, a multi-table atomic action, an outbox-enqueue assertion, and a governance / FSM-guard denial. Each fragment is copy-paste-ready and decodes cleanly against the runner's SessionSchema.

resources.title: Running and debugging
resources.description: How to run scenarios through the harness (bun test, the CLI vs REST transport switch), how to isolate a single scenario, how to read the runner's verbatim CLI echo + AssertionFailure path output, and how the empty-canon test DB is booted and torn down.
